response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software engineer with 14+ years of professional experience, but his background is fundamentally misaligned with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency software development in DLang at Sociomantic and peripheral hardware-adjacent work with robotic arms and NI LabWindows C/C++ components, but these fall far short of the hardware-software co-design and AI silicon performance engineering required at principal IC level.
strengths: Performance-conscious software engineering background, including low-latency systems handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Broad multi-language and systems-level experience spanning embedded contexts, C/C++, and diverse runtime environments demonstrating technical adaptability
AI-adjacent work in agentic infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq shows awareness of modern AI tooling ecosystems
concerns: No evidence of SoC or IP modeling experience, which is a non-negotiable core requirement for this role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, DNN training or inference optimization, or AI accelerator hardware — all central to the position's technical charter
Career trajectory (BS Computer Science, predominantly web and full-stack roles) does not approach the hardware and system architecture depth expected at principal level in this domain

key_factors.factor: Absent SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or DNN optimization background
